meaning: adj.危险的,不易对付的,精明的,狡猾的adv.非常地,极
examples: Given most newspapers' parlous finances, this seems implausible.
But parlous public finances may force politicians to face up to awkward choices.
Given the parlous state of Congo's judiciary, raising the number of prosecutions may not help.
Investors have been willing to put money into this sector, despite its parlous condition.
Miss Oliver said that Stott had "parlous" finances and his 24-year career was now "effectively over".
